long_description: An in-flight hydraulic leak reduces available fluid and pressure, threatening powered flight controls, landing gear actuation, or braking.
pilot_actions: Monitor pressure trend, conserve hydraulic demand, avoid unnecessary configuration changes, and plan landing with alternate extension procedures.

long_description: Hydraulic pressure drops in flight because of pump degradation or internal leakage, causing sluggish or partial system response.
pilot_actions: Reduce maneuver loads, maintain stable airspeed, confirm backup system status, and recover before further hydraulic degradation occurs.

long_description: Hydraulic fluid contamination causes erratic valve or actuator response in flight, leading to inconsistent control feel and unreliable subsystem actuation.
pilot_actions: Avoid rapid configuration changes, monitor for asymmetric response, keep the aircraft in a stable regime, and recover before contamination effects spread.

long_description: A hydraulic pump fails in flight, reducing available pressure margin and increasing dependency on remaining circuits or backup sources.
pilot_actions: Minimize high-demand maneuvers, confirm alternate pressure source status, and recover before additional hydraulic loads are required.

long_description: Hydraulic accumulator charge is lost in flight, weakening transient pressure support for braking, emergency extension, or actuator demand spikes.
pilot_actions: Avoid rapid configuration changes, expect degraded backup response, and brief for limited emergency hydraulic performance on landing.

long_description: Hydraulic reservoir quantity drops in flight, reducing sustained system endurance and increasing aeration or cavitation risk.
pilot_actions: Conserve hydraulic demand, avoid unnecessary system cycling, and recover before fluid loss degrades control or landing functions further.

long_description: A hydraulic isolation valve fails in flight, preventing proper segregation or backup routing between circuits during abnormal operation.
pilot_actions: Avoid aggressive maneuvers, assume reduced redundancy, and recover before another hydraulic fault removes remaining capability.

long_description: Hydraulic fluid temperature rises abnormally in flight, degrading fluid performance and increasing the chance of seal or component distress.
pilot_actions: Reduce system demand, avoid repeated high-load actuation, and land before overheating drives wider hydraulic degradation.

long_description: An actuator or servo develops excessive internal bypass in flight, causing sluggish response even though system pressure remains near nominal.
pilot_actions: Treat response as degraded, avoid precise or high-rate maneuvering, and recover while controllability margins remain adequate.

long_description: Emergency or backup hydraulic support becomes unavailable in flight, reducing recovery options if the primary circuit degrades later.
pilot_actions: Avoid any nonessential hydraulic demand, assume abnormal landing options are reduced, and recover before a second hydraulic fault occurs.
